author: blamouche
description: Run AI coding CLIs like Claude Code or Codex from a right sidebar terminal panel. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Benoit Lamouche
manifest.description: Run an AI assistant CLI like Claude from a right sidebar terminal panel, with a customizable runtime list.
manifest.name: Any AI CLI

name: Any AI CLI

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Install

**From the Obsidian community store** (recommended):

Open `Settings → Community plugins → Browse`, search for **Any AI CLI**, and click **Install** + **Enable**. The plugin's `main.js` embeds the Node proxy and Python PTY bridge sources and writes them next to itself on first start, so the standard three-file auto-install (`main.js` / `manifest.json` / `styles.css`) is enough.

**From this release** (manual install / pre-release testing):

1. Download `any-ai-cli-0.2.18.zip` below.
2. Unzip it inside your vault's plugin folder so the resulting path is `/PATH/TO/VAULT/.obsidian/plugins/any-ai-cli/`.
3. Enable the plugin in Obsidian → Settings → Community plugins.

No commands required. The plugin uses an embedded Python PTY bridge fallback so it works out of the box on macOS / Linux, and falls back to direct pipe mode on Windows.

### Optional — native PTY backend

For best terminal fidelity (full-screen TUI rendering, resize), install the native `node-pty` module after unzipping:

```bash
cd "/PATH/TO/VAULT/.obsidian/plugins/any-ai-cli"
curl -fsSLO https://raw.githubusercontent.com/blamouche/obsidian-any-ai-code/0.2.18/package.json
curl -fsSLO https://raw.githubusercontent.com/blamouche/obsidian-any-ai-code/0.2.18/package-lock.json
npm install --omit=dev
```

Then reload the plugin.

### Release assets

- `any-ai-cli-0.2.18.zip` — drop-in plugin folder (the 3 canonical files: `manifest.json`, `main.js`, `styles.css`).
- `manifest.json` / `main.js` / `styles.css` — also attached individually for Obsidian's auto-update protocol and tools like BRAT.
- `versions.json` — Obsidian uses it to find a backwards-compatible plugin version when the current `minAppVersion` is too high for the user's app.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/blamouche/obsidian-any-ai-code/compare/0.2.17...0.2.18

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Install

1. Download `any-ai-cli-0.1.46.zip` below.
2. Unzip it inside your vault's plugin folder so the resulting path is `/PATH/TO/VAULT/.obsidian/plugins/any-ai-cli/`.
3. Enable the plugin in Obsidian → Settings → Community plugins.

No commands required. The plugin uses an embedded Python PTY bridge fallback so it works out of the box on macOS / Linux, and falls back to direct pipe mode on Windows.

### Files in the plugin folder

This plugin spawns a child process for the embedded terminal, so it needs more than the usual three-file Obsidian bundle. Keep these in the plugin folder:

**Required — the plugin will not start without them:**
- `manifest.json` — Obsidian plugin metadata (id, version, minAppVersion).
- `main.js` — bundled plugin code.
- `pty-proxy.js` — Node child process that runs your CLI inside a PTY. Without it, every `Start` throws `Missing proxy script`.

**Recommended — the plugin loads without them but the experience is degraded:**
- `styles.css` — sidebar / toolbar / dropdown styling. Without it, the panel renders with raw browser defaults.
- `pty-bridge.py` — Python PTY fallback used on macOS/Linux when `node-pty` is not installed. Without it the proxy falls through to a plain pipe, which renders Claude/Codex full-screen TUIs poorly.

All five are attached individually below so you can hot-swap any of them without re-downloading the whole zip.

### Optional — native PTY backend

For best terminal fidelity (full-screen TUI rendering, resize), install the native `node-pty` module after unzipping:

```bash
cd "/PATH/TO/VAULT/.obsidian/plugins/any-ai-cli"
npm install --omit=dev
```

This requires `package.json` and `package-lock.json` (shipped inside the zip). Then reload the plugin.

`versions.json` is also inside the zip — it lets older Obsidian releases pick a backwards-compatible plugin version, but is not used at runtime.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/blamouche/obsidian-any-ai-code/compare/0.1.45...0.1.46

No commands required. The plugin uses an embedded Python PTY bridge fallback so it works out of the box on macOS / Linux, and falls back to direct pipe mode on Windows.

### Optional — native PTY backend

For best terminal fidelity (full-screen TUI rendering, resize), install the native `node-pty` module after unzipping:

```bash
cd "/PATH/TO/VAULT/.obsidian/plugins/any-ai-cli"
npm install --omit=dev
```

Then reload the plugin.

### Manual install / Obsidian auto-update

`main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` are also attached as standalone files for compatibility with Obsidian's plugin update protocol and tools like BRAT.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/blamouche/obsidian-any-ai-code/compare/0.1.40...0.1.41

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Install

**Easy install** — download `obsidian-any-ai-code-0.1.25.zip`, unzip it into your vault's `.obsidian/plugins/` folder, then inside the extracted `obsidian-any-ai-code/` folder run:

```bash
npm install --omit=dev
```

This installs the native `node-pty` dependency for your platform. Then enable the plugin in Obsidian → Settings → Community plugins.

**Manual install / Obsidian auto-update** — `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` are also attached as standalone files for compatibility with Obsidian's plugin update protocol and tools like BRAT.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/blamouche/obsidian-any-ai-code/compare/0.1.24...0.1.25
